narrative_blocks.header: Three sons served the Union in Civil War
narrative_blocks.body: Riverius III's sons Andrew Jackson (24th Maine Infantry, Sergeant), Sgt. Joseph Edgecomb (16th Maine Infantry, Co. A), and Warren (11th Maine Infantry, Co. G, Corporal) all served the Union in the Civil War. Three brothers across three Maine regiments. All three survived the war; Warren later died at Boston Navy Yard age 51 in 1898.

burial.plot_notes: Oak Grove Cemetery, Gardiner, Kennebec County ME (per Findagrave).

grandchildren.n: Mary Cornelia Houghton Hopson Zoll Peters

narrative_blocks.header: Civil War vet died working at Navy Yard
narrative_blocks.body: Warren served as a Corporal in Company G of the 11th Maine Infantry from August 1863 to February 1866. After three marriages and five children spanning thirty years, he died suddenly on May 23, 1898 at age 51 — collapsing while working as a machinist at the U.S. Naval Yard in Boston. Cause listed as 'loss of consciousness due to exhaustive work.' His third wife Annie received his Civil War pension.

burial.plot_notes: Blossom Hill Cemetery, Concord, Merrimack County NH (per Findagrave).

military_service.notes: Enlisted Aug 1, 1863. Honorably discharged Feb 2, 1866. (Per Findagrave VETERAN entry.)

career.notes: Died suddenly while working at the U.S. Naval Yard in Boston. Cause listed as loss of consciousness due to exhaustive work.

narrative_blocks.header: Husband and wife died two months apart 1949
narrative_blocks.body: Joseph Warren Hooker and his wife Annie Wilson Hooker died within two months of each other in 1949 — Joseph on February 4 and Annie in April. The compressed deaths suggested either coincidental simultaneous decline of a long-married couple, or perhaps Annie's grief-and-illness following Joseph's death. They are buried together at Olivet Gardens of Cypress Lawn Memorial Park in Colma California.

narrative_blocks.header: California migration with Illinois-born wife Annie
narrative_blocks.body: Joseph Warren Hooker migrated from the Maine-Connecticut family base westward to San Francisco California, where he and his wife Annie Wilson (born Illinois 1870) settled and raised their two daughters. Daughter Esther Hooker Dirking (1893-1965) and granddaughter Dorothy Beatrice Dirking Hanley (1916-1993) are also buried at the same Cypress Lawn cemetery in Colma — a four-generation California family cluster.

narrative_blocks.header: Born 1869, died San Francisco 1949 age seventy-nine
narrative_blocks.body: Joseph Warren Hooker was born December 13, 1869 and died February 4, 1949 in San Francisco California at age seventy-nine. He was the eldest documented son of his father Warren Hooker by Warren's first wife Elizabeth. The family's California migration was the western expression of the broader nineteenth-and-early-twentieth-century westward movement that drew Northeast-born Hookers across the continent.

burial.plot_notes: Olivet Gardens of Cypress Lawn Memorial Park, Colma, San Mateo County CA (per Findagrave).

sources.short: United States World War I Draft Registration Cards 1917-1918 (FamilySearch)

narrative_blocks.header: Three children married into the Edgecomb family
narrative_blocks.body: Of Riverius Jr's ten children, three married Edgecombs in close succession: daughter Mehitable married Joseph Edgecomb in 1834; son Samuel married Rhoda Edgecomb in 1823; daughter Emma Jane married the same Joseph Edgecomb in 1867 as his second wife after her sister Mehitable's death. Two Hooker sisters, one Edgecomb husband.
